danger_signals.visual_form: 2D sprite of uniformed soldier with weapon, distinctive Nazi military silhouette
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat; player must engage or avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Guard sprite orientation and movement speed changes, animated firing stance
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy has detected player; combat imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: 2D sprite of aggressive canine, animated lunging behavior
danger_signals.meaning: Fast melee threat, unpredictable movement pattern

danger_signals.visual_form: Wall texture with door frame but no passage, grid-based barrier
danger_signals.meaning: Progression blocked; key required or alternate route needed

danger_signals.visual_form: Health percentage readout at HUD, decreases when shot
danger_signals.meaning: Damage taken; continued exposure leads to death

safety_signals.visual_form: Item sprites scattered in levels, collectible objects
Weapon and ammo sprite pickups, visible on level ground
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; player can sustain offensive capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Wall tiles that slide back when activated, hidden chambers revealed
safety_signals.meaning: Safe spaces containing treasure, ammo, or health; rewards exploration

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinctive elevator sprite or door at end of level
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression; reaching this completes level and removes current threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Special collectible sprites, distinct visual markers
safety_signals.meaning: Increased survivability; gain additional life reserve

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Nazi insignia on uniforms, swastika references in environment (thematic to setting)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Guards in military uniform sprites; dogs as handler animals; implied boss enemies in finale levels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle Wolfenstein interior architecture; Nazi bunker aesthetics; military infrastructure theming per level

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: William 'B.J.' Blazkowicz as Allied spy protagonist
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player character sprite holding various weapons (knife, pistol, submachine gun, chain gun)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: First-person perspective represents Allied infiltration; objective is escape and sabotage missions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guards patrol in patterns, standing or walking slowly, no active engagement animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies maintain routine movement; do not attack until player detected or attacked

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guard sprites orient toward player, animate firing stance, visible projectile effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies rapidly engage; move toward player; continuous fire pattern; movement becomes erratic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy sprite disappears or collapses; replaced by collectible items (ammo, health, treasure)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Threat removed; area becomes temporarily safe; loot becomes available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Grid-based locked doors; wall textures block passage; elevator inaccessible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must find keys or alternate routes; backtracking required; exploration incentivized

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final large enemy sprite; enhanced animation frames; positioned at level end near elevator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss engages in complex attack pattern; higher health; more aggressive behavior; defeat required to progress

environmental_storytelling.example: Castle Wolfenstein interior design; Nazi bunker layouts; military infrastructure conveys setting as enemy stronghold
Guard distribution throughout levels suggests patrol patterns and defensive positions; dogs as breach hazards indicate security measures
Scattered valuables throughout levels imply wealth/resources seized by Nazis; collection rewards exploration and communicates player objective (espionage/sabotage)
Hidden chambers containing concentrated resources; activated by wall tiles; suggests clandestine supply caches within Nazi structure
Episode titles ('Escape from Castle Wolfenstein', 'Operation: Eisenfaust', 'Die, Führer, Die!') establish narrative of escalating Allied operations against Nazi leadership

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Static or slow movement in set patterns; no attack animation; weapon held but not aimed
Rapid sprite orientation toward player; movement acceleration; animated firing stance activation
Continuous firing animation; projectile emission; erratic movement attempting to close or maintain distance
Stationary or slow patrol; less structured movement than guards
Rapid lunging animation; fast movement toward player; unpredictable trajectory
First-person view; weapon sprite displayed in firing stance; can initiate combat
Enhanced sprite size; more animation frames; complex firing pattern; higher resilience to player attacks

notes: Wolfenstein 3D (Genesis 1994) uses rudimentary 2.5D graphics—3D environments with 2D sprite enemies—to communicate faction identity and world state. Nazi faction is established through visual theming (Castle Wolfenstein architecture, military uniform sprites) and implicit narrative (player as Allied spy). Danger is signaled through enemy presence, alert animations, and health depletion. Safety signals include health/ammo pickups and secret rooms rewarding exploration. World state changes are primarily conveyed through enemy behavior animations (idle vs. alert vs. combat) and environmental barriers (locked doors). The Genesis port maintains the original DOS visual language within technical constraints of the platform.
